clinical_criteria: CT or MRI of abdomen or abdomen/pelvis or chest/abdomen/pelvis with contrast within 3 months prior to or 1 month following visit
Hepatic venous pressure gradient >5mm Hg for portal hypertension
Ascites categories: None, Mild-Suppressed on medications, Moderate-Severe/Refractory
Macrovascular Invasion: No, Yes-minor branch, Yes-major branch
Metastasis: No, Yes-regional lymph nodes, Yes-distal
Tumor Morphology: Uninodular and extension<50% of liver, Multinodular and extension<50% of liver, Massive or extension 50% of liver
algorithm_summary: This phenotype algorithm identifies hepatocellular carcinoma (HCC) liver cancer stages using three staging systems: AJCC (stages I, II, IIIa, IIIb, IIIc, IVa, IVb), BCLC (stages A1, A2, A3, A4, B, C, D), and CLIP (stages 0-6). Step 1 involves collecting patient files from a single day to isolate onset-related data, obtaining required laboratory values (Albumin and Alpha F), and collecting radiology reports (CT/MRI with contrast) within a specific timeframe. The algorithm evaluates multiple clinical parameters including ascites, Child-Pugh class, ECOG performance status, extrahepatic invasion, hepatic encephalopathy, macrovascular invasion, metastasis, portal hypertension, and tumor characteristics (morphology, number, size) to determine appropriate cancer staging.
